literal: to do everything possible, to make every conceivable effort, or to use all available means to achieve a desired aim or overcome an obstacle
to change one's place of residence; to relocate from one house or dwelling to another
to affect someone emotionally to the extent that they cry
to change the rules, criteria, or targets in a way that makes it harder for someone to succeed or to meet expectations, especially after the process has begun
to produce a noticeable, measurable, or significant change in a situation, metric, or outcome
to improve one's social, economic, or professional status; to advance to a higher class, rank, or position
a great deal of fuss, commotion, or attention directed toward something trivial or unimportant
used to introduce a second, more extreme or less likely element, equivalent to 'let alone' (i.e., indicating that if the first thing is not true or possible, the second is even less so)
indicating that two or more things are essentially the same or have no significant difference; much the same
to manage or cope with a situation in an improvised, unskilled, or inelegant way, doing just enough to get by
to make a situation, issue, or set of facts less clear or more confusing, often by introducing irrelevant, ambiguous, or misleading information
a coarse simile used to describe a person or thing as extremely unattractive or homely
a derogatory or vulgar term for a person regarded as filthy, contemptible, or foolish
to be abruptly confronted by practical facts or constraints that force one to abandon unrealistic expectations, beliefs, or plans
an activity that is pointless, likely to fail, or one in which only a fool would engage; a sucker's game or losing proposition
a phrase used to instruct someone to keep silent about a matter or not to reveal information
an assertion that a murder, wrongdoing, or concealed crime will eventually be discovered or exposed
a group of people who excessively praise, flatter, or compliment one another, often implying self‑congratulation or lack of critical perspective
a rare or archaic exclamation indicating acute head pain, dizziness, extreme surprise, or disorientation; used to convey that one's head feels overwhelmed or struck
an ultimatum demanding that others accept the speaker's preferred course of action or leave/withdraw; indicating no willingness to compromise
